content: SGLT2 (sodium-glucose cotransporter-2) inhibitors are oral antidiabetic agents that block glucose and sodium reabsorption in the proximal renal tubule, causing urinary glucose excretion (60-80g/day). Agents include empagliflozin, dapagliflozin, canagliflozin, and ertugliflozin. Beyond glycemic control, SGLT2 inhibitors provide cardiovascular and renal benefits through multiple mechanisms: osmotic diuresis reduces preload and afterload, weight loss (2-3kg), blood pressure reduction (3-5 mmHg systolic), improved myocardial energetics, reduced inflammation, and decreased intraglomerular pressure. Indications now extend beyond type 2 diabetes to include heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF), heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF), and chronic kidney disease regardless of diabetes status. Major outcome trials (EMPA-REG OUTCOME, DECLARE-TIMI 58, DAPA-HF, EMPA-KIDNEY) demonstrated 14% reduction in MACE, 30% reduction in heart failure hospitalization, and 30-40% reduction in CKD progression.
